commit.message:
Add verbose logging and log-file support

New flags:
  -verbose    log every request/response with full headers + body preview
              (4 KiB cap, sensitive headers redacted: Authorization, Cookie, etc.)
  -log <path> tee all output to a file (append mode) in addition to stderr

Default mode now logs one line per request/response:
  -> GET    https://upstream/path
  <- 200    https://upstream/path  (14.2 KB, 42ms)

New file: logger.go — Logger struct wrapping log.Logger with:
  Printf / Fatal (always shown)
  Verbosef (verbose-only)
  LogRequest(req, bodySnapshot) time.Time
  LogResponse(resp, bodySnapshot, start)
  bodyDump, sortedKeys, humanBytes helpers

* fix: lazy-init india-pincode + serverExternalPackages to fix build

The `india-pincode` package reads `data/pincodes.json.gz` from disk at
init time. When `getIndiaPincode()` was called at module scope, Next.js
Turbopack evaluated it during `next build` page data collection, causing
"data file not found" because the data file wasn't in the bundled output.

Two fixes:
1. Add `serverExternalPackages: ['india-pincode']` to next.config.ts so
   the package is left as a runtime require() and can resolve its data
   file from node_modules/.
2. Make `getIndiaPincode()` lazy (called on first request, not at module
   eval time) as a defence-in-depth measure.

commit.message:
Implement import.source() dynamic expression for source phase imports

Add parsing, compilation, and runtime support for import.source(specifier)
which returns a Promise that resolves with the module namespace object.
This follows the same architecture as import() and import.defer():
parser recognizes the syntax, compiler emits a helper call, and the VM
dispatches to host_source_import_promise at runtime.

- Add host_source_import_promise VM method (loads module, resolves with namespace)
- Reject new import.source(...) with SyntaxError

commit.message:
fix: prevent scroll cascade clipping TabBar and AI Chat header on insert

When clicking 'insert to editor' in AI Chat, browser's scrollIntoView
cascaded through overflow:hidden containers (.workspace, .workspace-region),
shifting both TabBar and AI Chat header upward (upper half clipped).

Three-layer defense:
1. CSS: overflow:clip on .workspace-region (already on .workspace) -
   prevents browser from scrolling these containers at all
2. React onScroll handlers on both containers - resets scrollTop/Left
   to 0 as JS fallback for older WebKit without clip support
3. scrollEditorToSelection walks ancestors after adjusting .cm-scroller,
   resetting any unexpected scroll offset on overflow:hidden parents

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: don't include leading :: in captured alias target name

The template registry stores alias templates without the :: prefix,
so including it in the captured name causes lookup failures during
deferred alias materialization. The class alias path has no
lookup_alias_template guard, so a ::prefixed name passes the
isValid() check but fails at materialization time.

Skip the :: token without appending it to the name builder.

commit.message:
fix(tui): hierarchy navigation uses correct parent for children query

The task selector was using session mapping (Feature ID) instead of
current_parent_work_item (User Story ID) when drilling into children.
This caused sub-level items like Bug #274141 to not appear.

Key changes:
- Prioritize current_parent_work_item over session mapping when in sub-levels
- Add parent_stack for multi-level back navigation (Backspace/Ctrl+O)
- Show breadcrumb in title when viewing sub-level children
- Smart Backspace: pop hierarchy when filter empty, back to features at top
- Fix cache key to also use current_parent_work_item

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Git-style auto-fix workflow:
- 'Fix' button generates a code fix via Copilot CLI and stages it
- Staged fixes panel shows colored diff previews
- 'Unstage' removes a fix, 'Commit All Fixes' pushes to PR branch
- Fix buttons only appear on your own PRs (author detection via ADO API)

History restore:
- 'Restore' button on history entries reloads the PR with saved comments

fix: address PR #5 review comments

Code fixes:
- Server: graceful degradation on FC dir creation failure (no panic)
- Sandbox IDs: use Uuid::simple() for consistent fc- prefix format
- Machine config: wire memory_mb/vcpu_count via Firecracker REST API
- Concurrency: release global write lock before async operations

- KVM check: is_available() now verifies /dev/kvm exists and is accessible
- Path validation: use Path::components() instead of contains("..") in executor
- Vsock port: document coupling between config and executor constant
- Integration test: increase FC timeout from 30s to 75s

Doc fixes:
- CRATE_REFERENCE: correct JSON field names (content_base64, not data)
- CRATE_REFERENCE: fix state_dir/workspace_dir default paths
- CRATE_REFERENCE: fix dependency versions (hyper 0.14, hyperlocal 0.8)
